title: anthemis cretica subsp. gerardiana
intro: Anthemis cretica subsp. gerardiana, also known as Gerard's chamomile, is a subspecies of Anthemis cretica, a flowering plant in the daisy family. It is known for its calming and soothing properties, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Gerard's chamomile include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative effects, which can help to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, as well as promote relaxation and reduce stress. Gerard's chamomile can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote a good night's sleep and overall well-being.
